name: Down Home Dig-in Chili
recipeIngredient: 4 cups (7/8 pint) canned stewed tomatoes, drained (liquid reserved)
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt lard in a heavy large pot over medium heat.
2. Add the finely chopped onions, bell peppers, celery, and garlic. Cook and stir until the onions are translucent, about 10 minutes.
3. Using a slotted spoon, remove the vegetables and set aside.
4. Increase the heat to high. Add the beef and pork; season with salt and pepper. Cook until browned, stirring frequently, about 10 minutes.
5. Return the vegetables to the pot. Stir in the tomatoes, ale, chili powder, chilies, cayenne, and cumin.
6. Reduce the heat and partially cover the pot. Simmer for 2 hours, adding reserved tomato liquid if the chili appears dry. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
7. Uncover the pot and simmer until thickened and the meat is tender, an additional 2 hours.
8. Season the chili with hot pepper sauce. Serve with chopped green onions, grated cheddar cheese, sliced avocado, and sour cream.
Note:
Always chop meats in chili instead of using ground versions for better texture. Adjust the amount of hot pepper sauce to suit your heat preference. Taste before serving.
